title: Iran Suspends US Peace Talks as Israel Expands Lebanon Offensive
subhead: Tehran halted negotiations with Washington after Israel widened its Lebanon campaign, threatening to open new fronts and sending oil prices surging. Meanwhile, Ukraine's drone campaign squeezes Moscow's fuel supply, and India races to finalize a trade pact before US tariffs spike.

sections.heading: Iran Suspends Peace Negotiations with US
sections.body:
Iran's negotiating team suspended all dialogue with US mediators on Monday, according to the semi-official Tasnim news agency, citing Israel's expanding offensive in Lebanon as a breach of the ceasefire's core conditions. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared that 'the ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all fronts, including in Lebanon,' framing any Israeli operations there as a violation of the broader truce. The suspension came hours after a brief exchange of strikes between US and Iranian forces, with Iran targeting the base from which a US attack originated.

The move jeopardizes weeks of delicate diplomacy brokered by Pakistan and other intermediaries. Oil markets responded immediately: Brent crude surged past $97 per barrel, climbing more than 7% as traders priced in renewed risks to the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian officials and Tasnim explicitly discussed 'completely blocking' Hormuz and opening fronts at Bab el-Mandeb off Yemen, where Iran's Houthi allies could disrupt Suez Canal traffic. The war, launched by the US and Israel on February 28, has already caused global economic disruption through earlier Hormuz closures.
sections.whyItMatters: The suspension removes a diplomatic off-ramp from a regional war that has already sent energy prices soaring. If Tehran follows through on threats to Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb, global oil supply disruptions would intensify, with cascading effects on inflation, trade, and growth.

sections.heading: Israel Expands Lebanon Offensive to Beirut's Edge
sections.body:
Israel's military ordered evacuations for Beirut's southern suburb of Dahiyeh on Monday, with Defense Minister Israel Katz warning there would be 'no calm in Beirut' if Hezbollah continued its attacks on northern Israel. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu instructed strikes on 'terrorist targets' in the capital's densely populated southern district. The Israeli army declared all areas south of the Zahrani River—roughly 40 kilometers from the border, including Tyre and Nabatieh—as 'combat zones,' issuing new evacuation orders for towns north of the Litani River.

Hezbollah reported 21 strikes against Israeli forces over 24 hours, including drone attacks that the Israeli military confirmed killed one soldier and wounded three others. Israeli public broadcaster Kan estimated that Hezbollah's drone capabilities are now limiting 80% of Israeli troop assaults in southern Lebanon. The expansion to Beirut revives fears for thousands of displaced residents who had only begun returning after the April ceasefire.
sections.whyItMatters: The shift from southern Lebanon to Beirut marks a significant escalation that threatens to reignite full-scale conflict and further strains the Iran-US ceasefire framework.

sections.heading: Ukraine's Refinery Campaign Forces Fuel Rationing in Moscow
sections.body:
Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil infrastructure have created fuel shortages that have now reached the Moscow region. According to The Moscow Times, gas stations in Novaya Moskva began limiting gasoline sales to 60 liters per customer and diesel to 100 liters. Similar rationing has been imposed in Russian-occupied Crimea and parts of Ukraine. Russia also banned jet fuel exports from June 1 through November 30 to stabilize domestic supplies.

Bloomberg calculations show Ukraine attacked Russian refineries 16 times in May alone, with over 10 additional strikes on pipelines and storage facilities. By mid-May, Ukrainian officials said 10 refineries had been hit, forcing six to suspend operations and reducing Russian refining capacity by roughly 10%. While higher global oil prices from the Iran conflict have boosted Russian revenues—reaching an estimated €734 million per day in April—the Institute for the Study of War estimates repair costs and subsidies have offset much of the gain, costing Moscow approximately $4.7 billion in April alone.
sections.whyItMatters: Ukraine's asymmetric campaign is creating direct costs for Russian civilians and degrading Moscow's ability to benefit from war-driven oil prices, demonstrating how drone warfare can target an adversary's economic base.

sections.heading: South Korea's Exports Hit Four-Decade High on AI Chip Boom
sections.body:
South Korea's exports grew at the strongest annual rate in over four decades in May, driven by record chip sales from a global surge in AI investment. The trade-reliant economy posted growth not seen since the early 1980s, with semiconductor exports hitting all-time highs. Shares in Samsung Electronics and LG Electronics rallied Monday ahead of expected meetings between Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Korean tech executives, signaling potential tie-ups in AI and robotics.

The record performance comes despite broader global headwinds from the Iran-Israel war and trade tensions. South Korea's success illustrates how AI-driven demand is creating pockets of economic strength even as geopolitical risks weigh on other sectors.
sections.whyItMatters: The AI boom is creating a bifurcated global economy: tech-intensive exporters like South Korea are hitting record highs while energy-importing nations face inflation and currency pressure from the Middle East conflict.

sections.heading: India-US Trade Talks Enter Final Phase
sections.body:
A US Trade Representative team led by chief negotiator Brendan Lynch arrived in Delhi on June 1 for four days of negotiations aimed at finalizing an interim trade agreement. The urgency stems from a new US tariff framework under Section 301 set to take effect July 24, following a Supreme Court ruling that deemed previous reciprocal tariffs illegal. The US has reduced tariffs from 50% in August 2025 to 18% in February 2026, but Indian officials say the current 10% across-the-board rate remains preferable to accepting certain US conditions affecting oil purchases and agricultural imports.

US officials, including USTR Jamieson Greer and Ambassador Sergio Gor, have expressed optimism, with Gor saying only '1% of the deal remains.' But India's rupee has weakened past Rs 95 per dollar, and FDI inflows of $94.53 billion in 2025-26 translated to net FDI of only $7.65 billion, reflecting capital outflows. Finalizing the trade pact is seen as critical to restoring investor confidence.
sections.whyItMatters: With the Iran war pressuring India's currency and energy costs, Delhi has strong incentives to close the deal—but not at the cost of sovereignty over oil purchases. The outcome will signal whether US-India ties can weather economic nationalism on both sides.

sections.heading: France Seizes Russia-Linked 'Shadow Fleet' Tanker
sections.body:
French President Emmanuel Macron announced Monday that the French Navy, backed by the UK, intercepted a sanctioned tanker linked to the Russian oil trade in the Atlantic Ocean. The vessel, named Tagor, was boarded Sunday and ordered to head for the French mainland. Russia condemned the action as 'international piracy' and illegal.

The seizure marks the latest escalation in enforcement actions against Russia's 'shadow fleet'—vessels operating outside Western insurance and tracking systems to move Russian oil despite sanctions. European nations have intensified interdictions as Moscow seeks to circumvent price caps and export restrictions.
sections.whyItMatters: The enforcement action demonstrates continued Western resolve to squeeze Russian oil revenue, but also risks further confrontation with Moscow as the Ukraine war grinds on.

bottomLine: Iran's suspension of peace talks represents the most serious diplomatic rupture since the February war began, removing a potential path to de-escalation at a moment when Israel is widening its Lebanon campaign. Tehran's explicit threats to Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb send oil prices toward $100, compounding inflationary pressure worldwide. Ukraine's refinery strikes are creating real costs inside Russia, forcing fuel rationing near Moscow and degrading Moscow's ability to benefit from higher global prices. The India-US trade talks and South Korea's export surge illustrate two divergent economic trajectories: one driven by AI investment, the other by geopolitical pressure to reduce vulnerabilities before tariff deadlines hit.
